title: APIv2: Can do at most one request for rootless
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I can do only one request for rootless service, then the `podman.service` fails to start with `Error: unable to create socket unix:/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock: listen unix /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock: bind: address already in use`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Freshly booted machine: (everything is as expected)
```
 $ systemctl --user status podman.socket
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: active (listening) since Thu 2020-04-16 12:27:59 CEST; 3min 56s ago
     Docs: man:podman-api(1)
   Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)
   CGroup: /user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/podman.socket

Apr 16 12:27:59 z-hlmaschine systemd[1882]: Listening on Podman API Socket.

$ systemctl --user status podman.service
● podman.service - Podman API Service
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.service; disabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: inactive (dead)
     Docs: man:podman-api(1)
```

2. Do some request for rootless: (succeeds, all well and good)
```
$ curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.24/libpod/containers/json?all=true
[{"Command":["mongod"],"Created":1581845550,"Exited":false,"ExitedAt":-62135596800,"ExitCode":0,"Id":"1779ea8b42af50809fcf8fff0f570a43b4bdc6b6383e4480090d45ad991a83e3","Image":"docker.io/library/mongo:latest","IsInfra":false,"Labels":null,"Mounts":["/data/configdb","/data/db"],"Names":["mongo"],"Namespaces":{},"Pid":0,"Pod":"5933b46614e93ba8d2fe58f96124dc28acd1236ee4da95c598a88c327f31296b","PodName":"","Ports":null,"Size":null,"StartedAt":-62135596800,"State":"configured"},{"Command":null,"Created":1581845549,"Exited":false,"ExitedAt":-62135596800,"ExitCode":0,"Id":"1de315c48f30e3bd9a02f012af3371a480e016e6f53634493ff54b1e3b7bdd95","Image":"k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.1","IsInfra":true,"Labels":null,"Mounts":[],"Names":["5933b46614e9-infra"],"Namespaces":{},"Pid":0,"Pod":"5933b46614e93ba8d2fe58f96124dc28acd1236ee4da95c598a88c327f31296b","PodName":"","Ports":[{"hostPort":80,"containerPort":5000,"protocol":"tcp","hostIP":""},{"hostPort":27017,"containerPort":27017,"protocol":"tcp","hostIP":""}],"Size":null,"StartedAt":-62135596800,"State":"configured"},{"Command":["app.py"],"Created":1581885985,"Exited":false,"ExitedAt":-62135596800,"ExitCode":0,"Id":"f28d51a51239c0c2cb3e687dda2dbf9cf08edebba45c4f36624d0c753983aaad","Image":"localhost/mmarusak/tnuro:latest","IsInfra":false,"Labels":{"maintainer":"Clement Verna \u003ccverna@fedoraproject.org\u003e"},"Mounts":[],"Names":["tnuro_app"],"Namespaces":{},"Pid":0,"Pod":"5933b46614e93ba8d2fe58f96124dc28acd1236ee4da95c598a88c327f31296b","PodName":"","Ports":null,"Size":null,"StartedAt":-62135596800,"State":"configured"}]
```

3. Do another request (Doing the same, but it fails now)
```
$ curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock http://d/v1.24/libpod/containers/json?all=true
curl: (7) Couldn't connect to server
```

4. Check state of `podman.socket` and `podman.status`
```
$ systemctl --user status podman.socket
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: failed (Result: trigger-limit-hit) since Thu 2020-04-16 12:34:24 CEST; 12s ago
     Docs: man:podman-api(1)
   Listen: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock (Stream)

Apr 16 12:27:59 z-hlmaschine systemd[1882]: Listening on Podman API Socket.
Apr 16 12:34:24 z-hlmaschine systemd[1882]: podman.socket: Trigger limit hit, refusing further activation.
Apr 16 12:34:24 z-hlmaschine systemd[1882]: podman.socket: Failed with result 'trigger-limit-hit'.

$ systemctl --user status podman.service
● podman.service - Podman API Service
   Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/user/podman.service; disabled; vendor preset: enabled)
   Active: failed (Result: exit-code) since Thu 2020-04-16 12:34:24 CEST; 1min 12s ago
     Docs: man:podman-api(1)
  Process: 5209 ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman system service (code=exited, status=125)
 Main PID: 5209 (code=exited, status=125)
      CPU: 93ms

Apr 16 12:34:24 z-hlmaschine systemd[1882]: Starting Podman API Service...
Apr 16 12:34:24 z-hlmaschine podman[5209]: This function is EXPERIMENTAL.
Apr 16 12:34:24 z-hlmaschine podman[5209]: Error: unable to create socket unix:/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock: listen unix /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock: bind: address already in use
Apr 16 12:34:24 z-hlmaschine systemd[1882]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a
Apr 16 12:34:24 z-hlmaschine systemd[1882]: podman.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
Apr 16 12:34:24 z-hlmaschine systemd[1882]: Failed to start Podman API Service.
```

Note: Sometimes not even the first query succeeds and the error message from `podman.service` is slightly different:

```Failed to shutdown libpod runtime: Error shutting down container storage: A layer is mounted: layer is in use by a containerError: unable to create socket unix:/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock: listen unix /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock: bind: address already in use```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.9.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.9
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
$ podman info --debug

  cpus: 4

  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: z-hlmaschine
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0

    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 1935008
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.5.7-200.fc31.x86_64
  memFree: 12328919040
  memTotal: 16420462592
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.13-2.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.3+dev
      commit: bbd6f25c70d5db2a1cd3bfb0416a8db99a75ed7e
  swapFree: 0

        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.9.0-1.fc31.x86_64
```

comments.body:
No timeout is defined by default in podman.service systemd unit file, so the service is up for only 5 seconds.
```
$ podman system service --help
Run API service

Description:
  Run an API service

Enable a listening service for API access to Podman commands.


Usage:
  podman system service [flags] [URI]

Flags:
  -t, --timeout int   Time until the service session expires in seconds.  Use 0 to disable the timeout (default 5)
      --varlink       Use legacy varlink service instead of REST
```

You have to create your own service for your user :
```
$ cat ~/.config/systemd/user/podman.service
[Unit]
Description=Podman API Service
Requires=podman.socket
After=podman.socket
Documentation=man:podman-api(1)
StartLimitIntervalSec=0

[Service]
#Type=oneshot
Environment=REGISTRIES_CONFIG_PATH=/etc/containers/registries.conf
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman system service --timeout 0
TimeoutStopSec=30
KillMode=process

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
Also=podman.socket
```

Then reload and start the service
```
$ systemctl --user daemon-reload
$ systemctl --user start podman.service
```

> No timeout is defined by default in podman.service systemd unit file, so the service is up for only 5 seconds.

How is that related? It is socket activated, so it should not be a problem.
And the same timeout is in the root service which works just fine.

```
$ cat /usr/lib/systemd/system/podman.service | grep ExecStart
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman system service
```
@jwhonce wanna check that this is reproducible and indeed a bug as I am blocking cockpit-podman migrating from varlink to rest API on this.

@marusak The issue is a bug.  We exec podman when setting up for rootless.  That is currently orphaning us from systemd's socket activation protocol.  1.9 allows you one command while 2.0 currently allows none.  I'll research getting a fix for you.

/cc @giuseppe
Any update on this? We would like to get cockpit-podman with REST API into rhel-8.3, but we are slowly running out of time until we can actually make it.
@marusak can you try the latest version from git master?  I've used your reproducer and it seems to work fine here.  I can run curl multiple times and it always returns success.
Indeed, the current master does not have this problem anymore. Thanks!
